verdict_reasoning: SH2D2A is a soluble cytoplasmic adaptor protein containing SH2 domains. It functions in T-cell signal transduction and downstream signaling cascades entirely within the cytoplasm. It lacks a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or any documented membrane-anchoring mechanism. While it participates in intracellular signaling networks downstream of surface receptors, the protein body itself does not reach or remain stably associated with the plasma membrane.
